src_embedding_text: source: design test case id: tc-authentication-001 title: successful user creation and authentication module: user authentication source files or pages: 2, 6 covered methods: preconditions: - system is initialized with an empty database. - user has not previously registered. test data: - valid credentials: `email="user1@example.com"`, `password="securepass123!"`, `nome="john"`, `cognome="doe"`, `neighborhood="n123"` test steps: 1. user attempts to log in with invalid credentials (e.g., `email="nonexistent@example.com"`). 2. system detects user does not exist and triggers `usercreateprocedure`. 3. user submits registration data (`nome`, `cognome`, `email`, `password`, `neighborhood`). 4. `usercreateprocedure` sends "create new entry" to `storeddatadb`. 5. `storeddatadb` validates and stores the user data. 6. system redirects to `authenticationprocedure` with valid credentials. 7. user logs in with the newly created credentials. expected result: - user account is created successfully in `storeddatadb`. - user is authenticated and redirected to `home`. - `chatlist` and `neighborhood` associations are initialized for the user. unit: project:
